bio: John French Professor of Economics at Dartmouth College and a leading authority on the history and economics of United States trade policy.
name: Douglas A. Irwin

bio: American economist and financial historian who popularized academic finance and founded the Journal of Portfolio Management.
name: Peter L. Bernstein

bio: Great Lakes maritime historian who has written more than thirty books and teaches maritime history at Northern Michigan University in Marquette.
name: Frederick Stonehouse
top_work: The Wreck of the Edmund Fitzgerald

bio: Former Great Lakes merchant seaman from Alpena, Michigan, and a noted popular historian of Great Lakes shipping.
name: Mark L. Thompson
top_work: Steamboats and Sailors of the Great Lakes

bio: Professor Emeritus of History at Loyola University Chicago specializing in Midwestern, Civil War, and Great Lakes history.
name: Theodore J. Karamanski

bio: Economist, historian, and journalist; former finance and economics editor at The Economist and a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations.
name: Marc Levinson

bio: Professor of Political Science at the University of British Columbia, specializing in comparative politics and democracy.
name: Maxwell A. Cameron

bio: Historian and trade analyst, Ohio Eminent Research Professor at Ohio University, and former Chairman of the United States International Trade Commission.
name: Alfred E. Eckes

bio: Turkish-born economist and Ford Foundation Professor of International Political Economy at the Harvard Kennedy School.
name: Dani Rodrik

bio: American economist and 2008 Nobel laureate in economics, known for his work on new trade theory and economic geography.
name: Paul Krugman

bio: American economist and Harvard professor of political economy, a foundational figure in international trade theory.
name: Frank W. Taussig
top_work: The Tariff History of the United States

bio: Transnational environmental historian and professor at Western Michigan University, focusing on Canada and United States border waters and the Great Lakes basin.
name: Daniel Macfarlane

bio: American environmental historian, Frederick Jackson Turner Professor at the University of Wisconsin-Madison and a pioneer of American environmental history.
name: William Cronon

bio: American historian and longtime professor at New York University specializing in early American political and institutional history.
name: Carl E. Prince

bio: American political scientist specializing in the politics of international economic relations and a longtime professor of government at Harvard University.
name: Jeffry A. Frieden

bio: American author and energy and economic historian who won the 1992 Pulitzer Prize for The Prize.
name: Daniel Yergin

bio: Historian at Brock University and a leading authority on the Welland Canals, who helped found the Welland Canals Preservation Association.
name: Roberta M. Styran

bio: American author and longtime English professor at Miami University in Ohio, a prolific writer of Midwestern regional history.
name: Walter Havighurst

bio: Professor Emeritus and former Director of the Norman Paterson School of International Affairs at Carleton University, an expert on international negotiation.
name: Brian W. Tomlin

bio: Economist and energy strategy consultant who co-founded Cambridge Energy Research Associates with Daniel Yergin.
name: Joseph Stanislaw

bio: Leading international trade-law scholar and professor at Columbia Law School, a frequent author on the law of the World Trade Organization.
name: Petros C. Mavroidis

bio: Professor Emeritus of History at Brock University and a longtime co-author with Roberta Styran on the history of the Welland Canals.
name: Robert R. Taylor
